read_plan.section: The Ruins Claim Incident (~95 Years Ago)
read_plan.preview: The second near-war was precipitated not by trade competition but by the [[Ancient Ruins|ruins research]] that Auralis had begun funding formally in the preceding decades. An Auralis-sponsored expedition reached what it

The third near-war was [[Expansion Faction|Expansion Faction]] policy made explicit. The Faction had been building influence in [[The Council of Auralis]] for a generation; the Ashford Blockade was its first major test o

Auralis society leans toward a soft caste structure, where social standing is loosely tied to magical ability. This is not a rigid hierarchy of law — high-tier casters do not hold formal political office by virtue of the

Auralis embraces the full spectrum of [[Discipline|Disciplines]], including those that make other cultures uncomfortable. Experimentation with Mind and Soul disciplines is not openly banned and in certain scholarly or mi

The principal settlements of Auralis are [[Solhaven]] (the capital, built up coastal terraces on the western shore) and [[Emberfall]] (the interior research and agricultural center). Auralis has several additional port c
Solhaven approaches a million residents and is the most vertically stratified city in Solumora. Its terrace zones function as distinct social worlds stacked on each other.

Terravelle society values stability and efficiency above all. Where other cultures may chase magical prestige, Terravelle embraces the philosophy that the best spell is the one that accomplishes the task with the least e

Low to mid-tier casting is common and unremarkable. [[Grimoires]] here tend to favor Triangle and Square sigil shapes — fast, safe, reproducible, and affordable in [[Flux]]. [[Discipline|Disciplines]] with predictable fa

[[Terravelle]] and [[Auralis]] share a complicated relationship shaped by fundamentally opposed philosophies. Where Auralis pursues magical innovation and supremacy, Terravelle prizes restraint and function. The [[Equato

The principal settlements of Terravelle are [[Valdenmoor]] (the capital, in the central river valley), [[Ashford]] (the primary eastern port), and [[Greyveil]] (the northern waypoint at the edge of the settled belt). Doz

The numeric tier is the spell's **Flux handling requirement**. Each tier is roughly an order-of-magnitude jump in difficulty, capacity, stability, and precision. Every few tiers feels like a categorically different class
_Barely any Flux. The threshold most people never bother to formally test._
_A small, usable trickle. The first tier where deliberate spellwork is practical._
_The world average. The first tier where magic becomes genuinely useful in daily life._

Zakros sits directly on Solumora's equator, a belt of hostile terrain several weeks wide for any traveler moving north to south. It is not a single uniform landscape â€” dune fields, cracked salt flats, exposed rock shel

There are better routes and worse ones. No route is safe. The better paths â€” known to experienced guides, jealously kept, and sold for considerable [[Solumora|Watts]] â€” reduce crossing time and offer slightly more pr

The desert's ambient [[Flux]] distortion is the hard constraint on who can survive a crossing without specialist support. [[Control Tier|T0]] practitioners have no Flux capacity and cannot cast any of the protection effe

Zakros is not lifeless. Its ecosystem is built almost entirely on insects and arachnids, most of which are [[Flux]]-fueled â€” drawing ambient energy from the desert rather than relying solely on biological food chains.

The twenty members divide into three factions with distinct and occasionally competing goals. Alliances shift on specific votes but the factions themselves are stable — they represent genuine differences in what council

The largest faction, eleven members, organized around the council's defining long-term goal: the recovery and completion of whatever the pre-modern civilization in [[Equatorial Desert|Desert Zakros]] was building. The fa

Six members, organized around the straightforward goal of growing [[Auralis]]'s territory, population, and economic base. They are the most conventionally political faction — interested in trade routes, border security,

Three members who back [[Cassia]] with genuine loyalty: [[Maren Sollis]], an older woman who served under the previous queen and transferred her allegiance to the infant who survived her; [[Tovan Ashce]], a mid-tier cast

Guild certification follows a standard three-stage progression: apprenticeship, journeyman work, and certification assessment. The stages vary in length by trade and in difficulty by the tier grade being sought.

Most trades have multiple certification grades tied to [[Control Tier]] capability. The grades are trade-specific rather than universal: a T3 certification in structural sigil application means something different from a

Guild documentation requirements are not optional conditions on certification — they are themselves part of what is being certified. A practitioner who can perform the work but cannot maintain accurate field records duri

Guild membership in [[Terravelle]] carries weight beyond trade certification. The guilds provide dispute resolution, wage standards, seasonal assistance funds, apprenticeship placement, housing recommendations, and the i

Ashford is rougher than [[Valdenmoor]] in the way that port cities tend to be — more transient, more tolerant of the irregular, more oriented toward the transaction than the institution. [[Flux]] use here leans heavily t

The coastal territory north of Ashford — absorbed into [[Terravelle]] during the campaign in which Eddan's elder son died — has never fully settled into the kingdom's administration. The independent coastal communities t

Eddan considers Ashford one of the three non-negotiables of Terravellian security. Losing control of the eastern coast would cut off trade revenue that funds a significant portion of the kingdom's military and administra

Ashford's trade volume depends on three movement systems that overlap without merging cleanly. Bulk cargo arrives through the [[Maren Freight Chain]] and is redistributed through dock districts that can absorb schedule s

Surface ruins only. Broken foundations, partially buried structural elements, fragments of inscription on materials of unknown composition. The buildings, where they can be reconstructed from their remnants, were large a

No migration evidence. No record of where the population went. No graves. The absence of bodies is considered by most researchers to be the most significant single fact about the ruins — a civilization this large and thi

Among the dozens of ruin sites catalogued across the desert, three are categorically different from the rest. They are not simply broken-down structures — they are areas where the [[Flux]] field itself behaves abnormally
**Location:** Eleven days south of [[Halveth]], ~4 degrees north latitude.

Drest was close to the queen who preceded [[Cassia]] — close enough that when she died in childbirth, he was the person who moved first. Whether that movement came from grief, from love, from a sense of obligation, or fr

[[Cassia]] suspects Drest is her father. She has never confirmed it. She will never confirm it — there is no path to confirmation that does not cost more than the answer is worth, and the answer would not change anything

Drest leads the Culmination Faction within [[The Council of Auralis]] — the faction pursuing the recovery of whatever the ancient civilization in [[Equatorial Desert|Desert Zakros]] built. He believes, with the particula

Drest is one of three council members who back [[Cassia]] — and he is the loud one, which means his support is visible and therefore complicated. When he advocates for her in council chambers it draws attention to the fa

Expansion Faction members are not unified by any single belief about the ancient civilization or the nature of [[Flux]]. They are unified by the assessment that [[Auralis]]'s current position relative to [[Terravelle]] r

The Expansion Faction and the [[The Culmination Faction|Culmination Faction]] vote together on most major Council decisions and against each other on almost none. This is not ideological alignment — they want different t

The Ashford Blockade, approximately forty-five years ago, was the Expansion Faction's most ambitious early action — a systematic program of trade restrictions designed to extract strategic concessions from [[Terravelle]]

Eddan has been king long enough to have watched [[Auralis]] cycle through two queens and a regent before its current ruler settled in. He has outlasted advisors, survived three separate attempts to destabilize Terravelle

Eddan governs with a council he does not fully trust. This is not naivety — he is aware of their limitations and has mapped most of their competing interests with reasonable accuracy. Several have Auralis-adjacent sympat
Eddan's wife died some years ago. He has not remarried and shows no inclination to.

Eddan's assessment of [[Cassia|Queen Cassia of Auralis]] is respectful and, he believes, accurate: she is patient, she is competent, and she wants things that are incompatible with [[Terravelle]]'s continued independence
